channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Social Security Administration bans reading 'general news' at work
item.description: The Social Security Administration (SSA) has banned reading “general news” websites at work, The Hill has learned.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chrome Enterprise moves to better differentiate work, personal browsing
item.description: Google on Thursday unveiled new security controls for Chrome Enterprise, allowing IT departments to help employees better distinguish between work and personal browsing profiles.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: LibreOffice downloads on the rise as users look to avoid subscription costs
item.description: Interest in LibreOffice, the open-source alternative to Microsoft Office, is on the rise, with weekly downloads of its software package close to 1 million a week. That’s the highest download number since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: The two features each work to identify and warn you of a possible scam while it's unfolding, either over text or on a phone call.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: The LA Times’ new AI tool sympathized with the KKK. Its owner wasn’t aware until hours later
item.description: The Los Angeles Times’ billionaire owner, who unveiled an AI tool that generates opposing perspectives to be displayed on opinion stories, was unaware the new tool had created pro-KKK arguments less than 24 hours after it launched
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Without being there, Apple hits Mobile World Congress
item.description: If you overlook the introduction of the iPhone 16e with its own Apple-designed 5G modem along with the release of new iPads, Macs and processors, you might argue that Apple didn’t make an impression at this year’s Mobile World Congress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Data center vacancies hit historic lows despite record construction
item.description: Space supply in major data center markets increased by 34% year-over-year to 6,922.6 megawatts, considerable growth over the 26% increase in 2023 , according to CBRE research.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple has quietly updated its guidance on how to clean your AirPods, and suggests you buy a kit… from Belkin
item.description: You have an AirPods cleaning kit, right? Oh you don't? That's OK, Apple's got new some advice on that.
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: North Korean fake job hackers are going the extra mile to make sure their scams seem legit
item.description: Security researchers claim to have found multiple fake personas from North Korea, getting hired in the West.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: First Fujifilm GFX100RF images leaked in build-up to expected reveal – here’s what they tell us about the unique premium compact camera
item.description: Leaked images seemingly reveal the top and rear of the rumored Fujifilm GFX100RF, the anticipated 100MP medium-format compact.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google experiments with AI-only search as competition heats up
item.description: Google has launched an experimental search mode that replaces traditional links with AI-generated summaries, as it ramps up efforts to integrate AI amid growing competition. Currently, Google Search displays AI Overviews, which provide summaries above traditional search results.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to handle type erasure in advanced Java generics
item.description: Generics programming in Java enhances type safety and code reusability by allowing developers to define classes and methods using type parameters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The key new features in .NET 10
item.description: The first preview of the next long-term support release of .NET is now available. Like previous even-numbered releases, .NET 10 builds on features introduced in the current short-term release, with a focus on performance and reliability improvements. As .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JetZero teams with Delta to take the first blended-wing body commercial jetliner closer to reality
item.description: It’s tough enough bringing the first major redesign of a commercial jetliner to fruition—especially when it looks more like something out of The X-Files than a travel brochure.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: AI pioneers win the Turing Award, tech’s top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft launches genAI sales agents, also focuses on finance and supply chain
item.description: Microsoft is planning a string of new AI agents to automate mundane tasks and improve worker productivity. The company on Wednesday announced two AI-based sales agents that can autonomously follow up on sales leads.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ancient humans made tools from animal bones 1.5 million years ago
item.description: A new study says early humans were regularly using animal bones to make cutting tools 1.5 million years ago.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SolarWinds buys Squadcast to speed incident response
item.description: Observability provider SolarWinds announced this week it had signed an agreement to acquire San Francisco-based Squadcast and its incident response technology for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Electronic employee monitoring reaches an all-time high
item.description: Monitoring of both remote and in-office employees is at an all-time high, a trend highlighted by federal workers being told to report their weekly accomplishments. A study by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) found that 80% of companies are monitoring remote or hybrid workers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DeepSeek claims 545% cost-profit ratio, challenging AI industry economics
item.description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek has claimed its V3 and R1 models achieve a theoretical daily cost-profit ratio of 545%, highlighting cost implications for enterprises adopting similar models from other cloud providers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Palo Alto expands secure private 5G network partnerships
item.description: Palo Alto Networks is looking to expand the role SASE plays in securing private 5G networks by collaborating with additional partners to offer end-to-end communications protection.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How eBPF is changing container networking
item.description: “No man is an island,” wrote John Donne. But containers certainly are. They are created as islands in and of themselves, packaged with all they need to operate independently—code, dependencies, and runtime. This autonomy is great for security but not so great for networking.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office
item.description: Thomas Shedd, the commissioner of Federal Acquisition Service, said the decision is part of the President’s Workforce Optimization Initiative executive order. The post After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Investment research data breach exposes 12 million customers
item.description: A recent ransomeware attack targeted Zacks, an American investment firm, and a cybercriminal claimed to have stolen records for millions of customers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Brand new Alexa
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TrapC proposal to fix C/C++ memory safety
item.description: TrapC, a fork of the C language, is being developed as a potential solution for memory safety issues that have hindered the C and C++ languages. TrapC is a proposed C-language extension compiler intended to be implemented as a cybersecurity compiler for C and C++ code, said developer Robin Rowe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5
